lede: A look at November 14 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Claude Monet and Jawaharlal Nehru, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Israel
events.desc: Israel launches a major military operation in the Gaza Strip in response to an escalation of rocket attacks by Hamas.

events.title: Cyclone Forrest
events.desc: In poor conditions caused by Cyclone Forrest, Vietnam Airlines Flight 474 crashes near Nha Trang, killing 30.

events.title: Lech Wałęsa
events.desc: Lech Wałęsa, the leader of Poland's outlawed Solidarity movement, is released after eleven months of internment near the Soviet border.

events.title: France
events.desc: France conducts the Aphrodite nuclear test as 25th in the group of 29 1975–78 French nuclear tests.

events.title: Southern Airways Flight 932
events.desc: Southern Airways Flight 932 crashes in the mountains near Huntington, West Virginia, killing 75, including almost all of the Marshall University football team.

events.title: Theodore Maiman
events.desc: American physicist Theodore Maiman is given a patent for his ruby laser systems, the world's first laser.

events.title: Ruby Bridges
events.desc: Ruby Bridges becomes the first black child to attend an all-white elementary school in Louisiana.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: In England, Coventry is heavily bombed by German Luftwaffe bombers. Coventry Cathedral is almost completely destroyed.

events.title: Moby-Dick
events.desc: Moby-Dick, a novel by Herman Melville, is published in the USA.

events.title: Gottfried Kirch
events.desc: German astronomer Gottfried Kirch discovers the Great Comet of 1680, the first comet to be discovered by telescope.

births.name: Jawaharlal Nehru
births.line: Indian lawyer and politician, 1st Prime Minister of India

births.name: Boutros Boutros-Ghali
births.line: Egyptian politician and diplomat, 6th Secretary General of the United Nations

births.name: Park Chung Hee
births.line: South Korean general and politician, 3rd President of South Korea

births.name: Maria Cristina of Savoy
births.line: Queen of the Two Sicilies from 1832 to 1836

deaths.text: Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel, German philosopher, author, and academic
Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz, German mathematician and philosopher

observances.text: Children's Day, celebrated on the birthday of Jawaharlal Nehru (previously on November 20). (India)
Christian feast day: All Saints of the Carmelites
Christian feast day: All Souls of the Benedictine family
Christian feast day: Barlaam of Kiev (Eastern Orthodox Church)
